# Head to the `biomejs/biome` repository, and modify the source code in there.
title: useFragmentSyntax
description: Learn more about useFragmentSyntax
---

## Summary
- Rule available since: `v1.0.0`
- Diagnostic Category: [`lint/style/useFragmentSyntax`](/reference/diagnostics#diagnostic-category)
- This rule isn't recommended, so you need to enable it.
- This rule has an [**unsafe**](/linter/#unsafe-fixes) fix.
- The default severity of this rule is [**information**](/reference/diagnostics#information).
- Sources: 
  - Same as [`react/jsx-fragments`](https://github.com/jsx-eslint/eslint-plugin-react/blob/master/docs/rules/jsx-fragments.md)

## How to configure
```json title="biome.json"
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"useFragmentSyntax": "error"
			}
		}
	}
}

```
## Description
This rule enforces the use of `<>...</>` over `<Fragment>...</Fragment>`.

The shorthand fragment syntax saves keystrokes and is only inapplicable when keys are required.

## Examples

### Invalid

```jsx
<Fragment>child</Fragment>
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text">code-block.jsx:1:1 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/use-fragment-syntax">lint/style/useFragmentSyntax</a> <span style="color: #000;
